claim_boundary: The output is a current model-derived recommendation only; implementation and parity remain unproven until downstream evidence exists.

closure_rules.scope: current target revision and declared route only

forbidden_shortcuts.summary: Do not create a SkillGuard-owned alternate execution route.
Do not convert configured booleans, generated JSON, or progress into native pass evidence.
Do not skip required stages, checks, receipts, or blockers.

integration_claim_boundary: SkillGuard gates the target-native FlowGuard route; it does not execute a parallel controller or replace native model/test evidence.

native_check_inventory.summary: Run model-derived code structure recommendation tests.

native_route_bindings.source: FlowGuard canonical route registry and target contract source

not_parallel_route_proof.evidence_source: Canonical route registry, contract source, and may_define_* false gates
not_parallel_route_proof.summary: SkillGuard only validates the FlowGuard-owned route and cannot define an alternate successful execution path.

phases.summary: Confirm recommendation-only scope and name the source functional model.
Inventory FunctionBlocks, state, fields, side effects, config, public entrypoints, and validation boundaries.
Map cohesive target modules, single owners, facades, adapters, and observable leaf boundaries.
Run owner-specific structure tests and reject source-less, duplicate-owner, or hidden-boundary recommendations.
Return a recommendation and typed StructureMesh, MTA, or FieldLifecycleMesh handoffs without code edits.

quality_floors.summary: Prompt prose, progress, or generated records cannot replace native evidence.
Missing, stale, skipped, scoped, progress-only, or pass-with-gaps evidence cannot support broad closure.

required_evidence.source: FlowGuard native command receipt or direct target artifact
FlowGuard native command receipt or direct target artifact
FlowGuard native command receipt or direct target artifact
FlowGuard native command receipt or direct target artifact
FlowGuard native command receipt or direct target artifact

route_inventory.summary: Derive cohesive module, facade, ownership, and validation boundaries from a named functional model before code edits.

do_not_use_when: Existing code is already being refactored, behavior requirements are missing, or runtime/test parity is the actual question.
routes.summary: Derive cohesive module, facade, ownership, and validation boundaries from a named functional model before code edits.

runtime_lock_policy.applies_when: The flowguard-code-structure-recommendation skill is selected or delegated.
runtime_lock_policy.summary: The target-local contract, route, stage order, checks, evidence, and coverage matrix gate closure.

source_requirements.summary: Every target module and owner must derive from a named FlowGuard functional model.
The route recommends structure and hands existing-code work to StructureMesh; it does not refactor production code.
State, behavior-bearing fields, side effects, config, and public facades require explicit non-duplicated ownership.
Accept a source model and named responsibilities; produce FunctionBlock-to-module ownership, state/field/side-effect maps, facade plan, and validation boundaries; block source-less or dupli.
description: Use when a FlowGuard model should drive pre-code module and function boundaries, FunctionBlock ownership, field/state/side-effect owners, facade design, adapter boundaries, or.
Name the source model, FunctionBlocks, state, fields, side effects, and public entrypoints.
Route id: code_structure_recommendation; role: public_owner; native owner: code_structure_recommendation. This standalone FlowGuard satellite skill owns recommendation-only architecture.
Use before code when module split, function ownership, facade, adapter, field reader/writer, or validation boundary is unclear.
The target section 'Use When' must be represented by route, check, evidence, or closure coverage.
Do not invent modules before responsibilities; require one owner per state/field write, explicit public facade, and validation boundaries.
Edit .skillguard/contract-source.json, then regenerate derived contracts; SkillGuard checks recommendation inputs/outputs and cannot implement the proposed structure.
The target section 'Hard Gates' must be represented by route, check, evidence, or closure coverage.
Return evidence, failures, blockers, skipped_checks, residual_risk, claim_boundary, and typed_next_actions, plus a code structure diagram and ownership map.
Verify the real FlowGuard check engine and AGENTS.md managed record; never create a fake mini-framework.
The target skill description must be represented in the contract: Use when a FlowGuard model should drive pre-code module and function boundaries, FunctionBlock ownership, field/state/side-.
The target skill entrypoint and hard gates must be represented by the runtime contract.
Declared hard gates and required workflow steps must be represented by checks and closure blockers.
Do not perform existing-code refactors, invent behavior, or replace parity/alignment evidence; return missing models to model-first-function-flow.
The target section 'Do Not Use When' must be represented by route, check, evidence, or closure coverage.
The target section 'Output Requirements' must be represented by route, check, evidence, or closure coverage.
A too-large leaf must split or remain scoped; new/deepened models require template harvest closure.
Recommend cohesive target modules, single owners, facades, adapters, and observable leaf boundaries.
Record rationale plus StructureMesh, Model-Test Alignment, or FieldLifecycleMesh handoffs.
The target section 'Required Workflow' must be represented by route, check, evidence, or closure coverage.
